deployment_question: How does the model perform on sequence families or homologous clusters absent from training?
definition: Cluster sequences by homology, sequence identity, or a sequence similarity graph and assign complete clusters to train or test.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: enforces low sequence-similarity train overlap at the selected identity threshold

implementation_recipe: Compute pairwise sequence similarity or use a homology partitioning tool.
Build clusters or graph components at the chosen threshold.
Assign whole clusters to folds and report cross-split identity.
quality_gates: Report identity threshold, alignment method, and clustering algorithm.
Measure maximum train-test sequence identity after splitting.
Check label and family balance across folds.
failure_modes: Remote homology can remain below the identity threshold.
Large families can dominate split size.
Embedding similarity can reintroduce leakage if trained on labels or test sequences.

citations.title: GraphPart: homology partitioning for biological sequence analysis
citations.evidence: Partitions biological sequences to keep related sequences together while retaining more data than homology reduction.

citations.title: SpanSeq: Similarity-based sequence data splitting
citations.evidence: Introduces a sequence splitting method and reports random splitting inflates performance.
